other_observers: Kara Carragher found the bird, I was birding with her.; We found it. It was re-found the following day by Bill Maynard, Mark Peterson, Brad Steger, and Bryan Patrick in the evening.
specific_location: Canon City Riverwalk. Along Bluff Trail, between Sells and Raynolds Ave.
description: Kara spotted a warbler, that she quickly identified as a Mourning Warbler. I looked at the bird and agreed. The bird had a gray head with no hint of any white eye arches or eye ring. It also had a black band on the lower breast, above the all yellow underparts. See photos.Behaviors: Staying very low and in the shade.Call: It made a chip, it sounded a bit different then MacGillivray's Warbler, though I'm not sure how to describe it.Plumage: adult alternate plumage
photographer: Photographer: Brandon K Percival, Brandon K Percival
